decisions.rationale: The observation's base_record_id (corridor_queensboro-bridge-201110) already exists in the registry with identical payload — same corridor_name ('Queensboro Bridge'), same street ('Queensboro Bridge'), same boroughs (Queens, Manhattan), and same description referencing 16,000 bus trips a day. The observation resolves to its own existing registry record; per rules, when the closest match is the observation's own base_record_id, the decision is 'new' (already resolved).

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Queensboro Bridge (QBB) is a major bus corridor – 16,000 bus trips a day, including about 8,000 local riders – 110 buses during PM rush hour or one bus every 30 seconds

proposed_relations.description: Both records describe the same real-world corridor: the Queensboro Bridge as a major bus corridor connecting Queens and Manhattan. They share the same corridor_name ('Queensboro Bridge'), same street ('Queensboro Bridge'), same boroughs (Manhattan and Queens), and consistent descriptions of a major bus corridor connecting the two boroughs. They differ only in source documents (201110_qbb_approach_summary vs 201110_qbb_summary_recommendations) and minor description phrasing.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Queensboro Bridge (QBB) is a major bus corridor – 16,000 bus trips a day
